name: Sundowners Bar & Grill
description: Iconic West End waterfront restaurant serving fresh seafood and Caribbean favorites. Best sunset views on the island.
insiderTip: Grab a dock seat by 4:30pm for sunset.
addressDescription: Main road in West End, on the water

name: Cannibal Cafe
description: The best Mexican food on Roatan with huge burritos, tacos, quesadillas, and chimichangas. A beloved West End institution.
insiderTip: The banana pancakes are legendary.
addressDescription: Half Moon Bay road, West End

name: Roatan Oasis
description: Fine dining restaurant with an internationally inspired ever-changing menu featuring craft cocktails, ribs, gnocchi, and curries.
insiderTip: Try the chipotle ribs and blue cheese gnocchi.

name: Calelu's
description: The place to go for authentic Honduran baleadas in West End. Huge portions at incredibly affordable prices.
insiderTip: Get the loaded baleada for under $2 - best deal on the island.

name: Anthony's Chicken
description: Rosie cooks up some of the best jerk chicken on the island along with fresh seafood, grilled sausages, and tasty ceviches.
insiderTip: The jerk chicken is a must - pair it with a cold local beer.

name: Creole's Rotisserie Chicken
description: Famous for lightly spiced, juicy rotisserie chicken and one of the best breakfast menus on the island.
insiderTip: Come for breakfast - it's a hidden gem most people miss.

name: Pazzo Italian Restaurant
description: Charming Italian spot serving the best handmade pasta on the island. BYOB and intimate seating makes it a special night out.
insiderTip: Reservations are essential - seating is very limited. Bring your own wine!
addressDescription: West End, near Half Moon Bay

name: The Stowaway Gastrobar
description: Family-run gastropub with a rotating menu catering to meat lovers and vegans alike. Features vegan tacos, falafel salad, and creative pastas.
insiderTip: Check their daily specials board - the menu changes constantly.

name: Mila del Mar
description: Upscale dining at The Beach House with gorgeous Half Moon Bay views, amazing staff, and creative seafood dishes with craft cocktails.
insiderTip: Sunset dinner on the second floor terrace is unforgettable.
addressDescription: The Beach House, Half Moon Bay, West End

name: Blue Elephant Thai
description: Popular Thai restaurant with lovely water views and authentic dishes that keep locals and visitors coming back.
insiderTip: The pad thai with lobster is the splurge-worthy order.

name: Crisp Roatan
description: Go-to spot for tasty salads and affordable poke bowls in a colorful, Instagram-worthy shop filled with vibrant decor.
insiderTip: The poke bowls are fresh and perfectly portioned for a light lunch.

name: Sandy Buns Bakery
description: Voted the #1 best restaurant in West End by locals. Famous bakery and breakfast spot in the heart of West End.
insiderTip: Get there early - the fresh baked goods sell out fast.

name: Cafe de Palo
description: Gorgeous coffee shop in the Mangrove Bight section of West End with immaculate vibes and excellent breakfast options.
insiderTip: The atmosphere alone is worth the visit - perfect for remote work.

name: Bean Crazy
description: Popular coffee shop right across from Half Moon Bay serving sandwiches, smoothies, and excellent espresso drinks.
insiderTip: Try one of their iced smoothies after a morning dive.
addressDescription: Across from Half Moon Bay, West End

name: Argentinian Grill
description: Steakhouse specializing in wood-fired grilled steaks and seafood. A carnivore's paradise in West End.
insiderTip: The ribeye is perfectly grilled every time.

name: Silversides
description: Gastropub run by a professional chef with rotating specials from lamb curries to lobster pot pie. Great for brunch and upscale dinner.
insiderTip: The lobster pot pie is legendary - ask if it's on the specials board.

name: Ipanema Beach Bar & Grill
description: Waterfront restaurant serving fresh Mexican food and strong margaritas with stunning ocean views.
insiderTip: Grab a waterfront table for the best margarita experience.

name: Tong's Thai Kitchen
description: Beachfront Thai restaurant with spicy noodle dishes and creative Honduran twists like lobster pad thai and squid curry.
insiderTip: The lobster pad thai is worth every lempira.

name: Thirsty Turtle
description: Lively beachfront bar and restaurant great for casual meals, sunset drinks, and live music. Famous for their pizza.
insiderTip: Their pizza spot on the deck is the perfect casual dinner.

name: Naboo Restaurant
description: Exquisite local cuisine with stunning ocean views in West Bay. A refined Caribbean dining experience.
insiderTip: Ask for the seafood special of the day.

name: La Palapa Beach Bar & Grill
description: Beachfront restaurant at Infinity Bay offering a diverse menu with sushi nights and tropical cocktails.
insiderTip: Don't miss sushi night - it's the best deal on the beach.

name: Ibagari Sea Salt Restaurant
description: Fine dining bistro known for its fresh fish and USDA Prime American Beef. The most upscale restaurant on West Bay.
insiderTip: The tasting menu is a splurge but worth every penny.

name: Hangover Hut
description: Canadian-run beachfront spot in West Bay serving surprisingly good poutine and comfort food. Often has live music.
insiderTip: Yes, the poutine in the Caribbean is actually amazing.

name: La Placita
description: Casual collection of food stands offering everything from pupusas to tacos to gelato. Perfect for a quick bite after the beach.
insiderTip: Try a pupusa from one stand and gelato from another - best cheap eats.

name: Vintage Pearl
description: French-Caribbean fine dining at Bananarama with an extensive wine cellar and daily changing menu of fresh seafood and USDA beef.
insiderTip: The wine selection is unmatched on the island - ask the sommelier.

name: Celeste's Island Cuisine
description: Spectacular outdoor restaurant in West Bay featuring romantic lobster tail dinners and live music every Thursday.
insiderTip: Thursday night live music with lobster is the island's best date night.

name: Bite on the Beach
description: Informal deck restaurant overlooking the turquoise Caribbean Sea serving fresh lunch and dinner in West Bay.
insiderTip: Sit on the upper deck for the best ocean views.

name: Dragonfly Sushi & Fusion
description: Delicious and affordable sushi rolls with tasty craft cocktails. Caribbean-Asian fusion at its best in Sandy Bay.
insiderTip: The specialty rolls are creative and beautifully presented.
addressDescription: Coral Stone Shopping Mall, Sandy Bay

name: The Sunken Fish at Tranquilseas
description: High-end restaurant at Tranquilseas Eco Lodge with sustainable seafood, rooftop terrace, and the best sunset views in Sandy Bay.
insiderTip: Book the rooftop terrace table for sunset - you won't regret it.

name: Bambu Sushi & Fusion
description: Excellent dining spot in Sandy Bay with a fusion menu mixing Caribbean and Asian cuisine. Creative cocktails.
insiderTip: The Caribbean-Asian fusion rolls are unlike anything else on the island.

name: Anthony's Key Resort Restaurant
description: Resort restaurant offering buffet breakfasts, poolside snacks, and themed dinners with excellent sea views.
insiderTip: The Thursday night seafood buffet is legendary among locals.

name: The Beach Grill at Blue Bahia
description: Right on the water with outstanding burgers and seafood dishes. Open daily for breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
insiderTip: The burger with a Caribbean twist is the sleeper hit on the menu.

name: Gio's Restaurant
description: World-renowned restaurant and one of the oldest in French Harbour. Famous for Caribbean King Crab and outstanding seafood with Italian flair.
insiderTip: The king crab is the reason this place exists - don't order anything else.

name: Romeo's Restaurant
description: Elegant waterfront restaurant with outstanding wood oven pizza, great seafood dinners, and steaks. Indoor and outdoor dining.
insiderTip: The wood-fired pizza rivals anything in Italy.

name: Herby's Sports Bar & Grill
description: Arguably the best sports bar in Honduras with an all-American setting, great burgers, appetizers, and big-screen TVs.
insiderTip: Come for game day - the wings and atmosphere can't be beat.

name: BJ's Backyard
description: Established 25+ years ago, this over-the-water restaurant specializes in island-style food with conch burgers, shrimp, and ice-cold drinks.
insiderTip: The conch burger is the signature dish - don't leave without trying it.

name: Baleadas Express
description: Authentic Honduran street food in Coxen Hole serving traditional baleadas, tajadas, and local favorites at great prices.
insiderTip: The carne asada baleada is the best on the island.

name: Bojangles
description: Popular fast food spot in Coxen Hole serving fried chicken, biscuits, and quick comfort food favorites.
insiderTip: A familiar taste if you're craving something American.

name: Brazilian Steakhouse Roatan
description: New all-you-can-eat Brazilian steakhouse with a full salad bar, table-side carved meats, and a local beer included for $47.
insiderTip: Come hungry - the all-you-can-eat deal is incredible value.
addressDescription: Across from Coconut Tree Plaza, West End

name: Babalu's Bar & Grill
description: Fun restaurant and bar right on the sand at Foster's West Bay Beach serving American food and island favorites.
insiderTip: The beachfront setting makes everything taste better.

name: Sundowners Beach Bar
description: The beating heart of West End nightlife planted right on the beach. Happy hour specials, theme nights, and the best sunset view on the island.
insiderTip: Happy hour starts at 4pm - the rum punch is legendary.

name: Booty Bar
description: Waterfront bar by day that transforms into a pumping nightclub after dark. DJs, dancing, and strong drinks.
insiderTip: It's chill during the day but gets wild after 10pm.

name: Frank's
description: Late-night bar and club with DJ music, dance floor, and a loyal following of locals and visitors. The last stop of the night.
insiderTip: This is where the night ends up - usually after midnight.

name: Tequila Jacks
description: Hot new bar at Brisas del Mar hotel with craft cocktails, tequila flights, and a pool-side atmosphere.
insiderTip: The tequila flight is the best way to start your evening.
addressDescription: Brisas del Mar Hotel, West End

name: Bariloche
description: New wine bar and restaurant with live music on Wednesday and Friday nights. Great steaks and an impressive wine selection.
insiderTip: Wednesday and Friday are live music nights - arrive early for a good seat.

name: Blue Marlin
description: The island's most popular dance club with a pumping dance floor, generously poured drinks, and a mixed crowd of locals and travelers.
insiderTip: This is THE party spot past 10pm - the dance floor gets packed.

name: Eagle Ray's Bar
description: Laid-back waterfront dive bar popular with the expat and diver crowd. Cold beers, pool table, and great conversation.
insiderTip: The cheapest cold beers in West End - perfect after a dive.

name: Happy Harry's
description: Popular seafood restaurant and bar with karaoke nights that draw a fun crowd. Great drinks and a jovial atmosphere.
insiderTip: Karaoke night is surprisingly fun - even if you can't sing.

name: Beachers Bar & Grill
description: Directly on West Bay Beach sand, this family-run bar has been serving for over 15 years. Famous for Sunday Fun-Day sessions.
insiderTip: Sunday Fun-Day is the best day party on the island.
addressDescription: On the sand, West Bay Beach

name: Land's End Bar
description: Beachfront bar at the quieter end of West Bay Beach with cold drinks, a relaxed vibe, and stunning views.
insiderTip: Walk to the far end of the beach for a more peaceful drink.
addressDescription: South end of West Bay Beach

name: Blue Bahia Beach Bar
description: Beachfront bar at Blue Bahia Resort with weekly specials, cocktails, and a chill Sandy Bay atmosphere.
insiderTip: Check the weekly specials board - different theme each day.

name: Jolly Roger Bar
description: Pirate-themed bar in West End with fun decor, strong rum drinks, and a lively crowd every night.
insiderTip: Try the Captain's Grog - their signature rum cocktail.

name: San Simon Beach Club
description: Stunning beach club in West Bay combining beachside luxury with vibrant social settings and exquisite dining options.
insiderTip: Get a day pass and enjoy the lounge chairs with bottle service.

name: Coconut Bar
description: Local watering hole in Coxen Hole popular with residents. Cold beer, local rum, and authentic island atmosphere.
insiderTip: This is where the locals go - great for an authentic experience.

name: Tranquil Seas Bar
description: Rooftop bar at Tranquilseas Eco Lodge with stunning sunset views and craft cocktails in Sandy Bay.
insiderTip: Time your visit for golden hour - the rooftop views are incredible.

name: Romeo's Wine Bar
description: Wine bar extension of Romeo's Restaurant with an impressive selection of international wines and elegant waterfront seating.
insiderTip: Ask for the wine flight - perfect for trying local and international options.

name: La Palapa Bar at Infinity Bay
description: Beach bar at Infinity Bay Resort with tropical cocktails, beachfront seating, and regular event nights.
insiderTip: Their Sunday beach party is a West Bay institution.

name: Roatan Island Brewing Co.
description: Craft brewery producing small-batch beers inspired by island life. Tasting room open daily with rotating taps.
insiderTip: Try the West End IPA - brewed with local ingredients.

name: The Mermaid Bar
description: Colorful waterfront bar in West End with handcrafted cocktails and a fun, eclectic atmosphere.
insiderTip: The bartender's custom cocktails are always worth trying.

name: Coco Lounge
description: Upscale beachfront lounge in West Bay with premium cocktails, chill music, and comfortable beachside seating.
insiderTip: Perfect for a sunset cocktail in a more refined setting.

name: Half Moon Bay Bar
description: Casual open-air bar right on Half Moon Bay with cheap drinks and a dive-shop crowd that keeps it real.
insiderTip: The cheapest rum and cokes in the bay.

name: The Pirate Bar
description: Fun-themed bar with skull-and-crossbones decor, strong rum cocktails, and a late-night party vibe.
insiderTip: Their rum bucket is enough to share - or not.

name: Lighthouse Bar
description: Elevated bar overlooking the West End shore with panoramic views, cold beer, and fresh cocktails.
insiderTip: Climb up for the best elevated view in West End.

name: Mega Plaza Food Court
description: French Harbour's main mall with restaurants, coffee shops, and casual dining in a modern air-conditioned setting.
insiderTip: A good option for air conditioning and variety on a hot day.

name: Paya Bay Beach Bar
description: Secluded bar at the eastern end of the island with a bohemian, clothing-optional vibe and stunning natural beauty.
insiderTip: It's worth the drive east for a completely different Roatan experience.

name: Coconut Tree Divers
description: PADI 5-Star Instructor Development Center in Half Moon Bay. Roatan's only TEC Diving Center with courses from beginner to instructor.
insiderTip: Book the early morning dive for the calmest water and best visibility.

name: Roatan Divers
description: Multi-award-winning PADI 5-Star IDC Eco Center and TEC Center. Known for boutique diving with small groups in the heart of West End.
insiderTip: Their eco-friendly approach means healthier reefs and better dives.

name: Sun Divers Roatan
description: Roatan's first-ever PADI Eco Center run by husband-wife team Shannon and Natalie. Small groups of 8-10 max on two custom boats.
insiderTip: The small group size means more personal attention and better dives.

name: West End Divers
description: The longest continuously operating dive shop in West End. PADI 5-Star Dive Resort offering daily excursions and full certification courses.
insiderTip: Their experience shows - the dive masters know every reef like the back of their hand.

name: Roatan Dive Center
description: Dive center offering both North and South shore diving from their West End and Dixon Cove locations. Great for experiencing different reef systems.
insiderTip: Ask about their South Shore dives - totally different reef experience.

name: Octopus Dive School
description: Boutique PADI dive center focused on small groups and personalized instruction. Perfect for beginners and nervous first-timers.
insiderTip: If it's your first time diving, this is the shop to go to.

name: Native Sons Diving
description: The longest-running dive shop in West End under the same local ownership. Alvin Jackson brings 60+ years of reef knowledge.
insiderTip: Ask Alvin for stories - he's been diving these reefs for 60 years.

name: Reef Gliders
description: Popular West End dive shop offering fun dives and PADI courses with experienced instructors and well-maintained equipment.
insiderTip: Their night dive is one of the best on the island.

name: West Bay Divers
description: PADI 5-Star dive shop with two locations right on West Bay Beach. Perfect for beach-based diving and snorkeling.
insiderTip: Shore diving right off West Bay Beach is incredible for beginners.

name: Seagrape Dive Shop
description: Over 15 years of local reef expertise at Seagrape Plantation Resort. Known for personalized attention and reef conservation.
insiderTip: Their reef knowledge is unmatched after 15+ years in the same spot.

name: Dockside Dive Center
description: Full-service PADI 5-Star dive center at CoCo View Resort offering world-class wall diving on the South Shore.
insiderTip: The South Shore wall dives here are some of the best in the Caribbean.

name: Luna Beach Dive Shop
description: Laid-back and friendly dive shop at Luna Beach Resort, steps from both West End and West Bay. Great for convenience.
insiderTip: Perfect location if you want to dive in the morning and beach in the afternoon.

name: Blue Island Divers
description: Secluded boutique B&B and scuba diving destination in Sandy Bay. Intimate diving experience away from the West End crowds.
insiderTip: Stay here for the full immersive dive-and-stay experience.

name: Island Snorkel Tours
description: Guided snorkel tours exploring the reef right off West Bay Beach. No diving experience necessary - perfect for families.
insiderTip: Great for kids who want to see the reef without scuba gear.

name: Roatan Ocean Adventures
description: Snorkel, wildlife, and boat tours exploring Roatan's marine life including sea turtles, dolphins, and vibrant coral.
insiderTip: The turtle snorkel tour is magical for all ages.

name: Deep Blue Freediving
description: Professional freediving instruction and guided dives. Explore the reef on a single breath in the crystal-clear Caribbean.
insiderTip: Even the beginner course is a life-changing experience.

name: Bananarama Dive Center
description: Dive center at Bananarama Resort on West Bay Beach offering easy shore diving and boat trips along the reef.
insiderTip: Shore dive right off the resort beach - the reef is steps away.

name: Paradise Beach Dive Center
description: PADI 5-Star Instructor Development Center at Paradise Beach Hotel on West Bay Beach. Full range of courses and daily dives.
insiderTip: Their Discover Scuba program is one of the best intro experiences.

name: Turquoise Bay Dive & Beach Resort
description: Dive resort in Sandy Bay with PADI courses, daily boat dives, and beautiful shore diving right from the dock.
insiderTip: The house reef is one of the best shore dives on the island.

name: Mayan Divers at Mayan Princess
description: Dive shop at the Mayan Princess Resort offering convenient reef access and PADI courses for resort guests and walk-ins.
insiderTip: If you're staying at Mayan Princess, the dive shop is right downstairs.

name: Anthony's Key Resort Dive Center
description: Full-service dive center at Anthony's Key Resort with dolphin dives, reef dives, and PADI courses in Sandy Bay.
insiderTip: The dolphin dive experience is unlike anything else in the Caribbean.

name: Roatan Reef Tours
description: Guided snorkel and fishing tours departing from West End. Explore pristine reef sections and catch your lunch.
insiderTip: The combo snorkel-fishing trip is the best value.

name: Infinity Bay Dive Center
description: Dive shop at Infinity Bay Resort providing easy access to West Bay's renowned reef. PADI courses and daily boat dives.
insiderTip: The reef right off Infinity Bay is teeming with marine life.

name: Oak Ridge Snorkel Adventure
description: Guided snorkel tours through the mangroves and reefs around Oak Ridge. A unique off-the-beaten-path experience.
insiderTip: The mangrove snorkel is a completely different world from the reef.

name: Glass Bottom Boat Tours
description: See the reef without getting wet on a glass bottom boat tour departing from West Bay Beach. Great for all ages.
insiderTip: Perfect if you want to see the reef but don't want to snorkel.

name: Blue Marlin Night Club
description: Roatan's biggest and loudest dance club with DJs spinning until 3am. The go-to spot when you want to dance all night.
insiderTip: Things don't really start until after midnight.

name: Sundowners Live Music Nights
description: Sundowners transforms into the island's best live music venue on Tuesday and Friday nights with local and visiting artists.
insiderTip: Friday night reggae sets are the best free show on the island.

name: Booty Bar Nightlife
description: Waterfront bar that transforms into a dance club after dark with a DJ, strong drinks, and a crowd that comes to dance.
insiderTip: It gets wild after 10pm - especially on weekends.

name: Frank's Night Club
description: Late-night institution with DJ music and a dance floor that keeps going until the early hours. Where the night ends.
insiderTip: This is the after-party spot - stumble in around 1am.

name: Happy Harry's Karaoke Nights
description: Lively karaoke bar where visitors and locals belt out tunes over strong drinks and great seafood.
insiderTip: Sign up early - the list fills up fast on busy nights.

name: Infinity Bay Beach Party
description: Sunday beach parties at Infinity Bay Resort with DJs, dancing on the sand, and tropical cocktails flowing all day.
insiderTip: The Sunday party is a West Bay tradition - don't miss it.

name: West Bay Beach Bonfire
description: Regular beach bonfire nights on West Bay Beach with fire dancers, acoustic music, and a magical atmosphere under the stars.
insiderTip: The fire dancers are mesmerizing - find a spot in the sand early.

name: RoaCrawl Bar Crawl
description: Organized bar crawl through West End's best nightlife spots with guides, drink specials, and a guaranteed good time.
insiderTip: Book in advance - it's the best way to experience West End nightlife in one night.

name: Roots Reggae Bar
description: Authentic reggae bar in West End with Garifuna music, local DJs, and a true Caribbean nightlife experience.
insiderTip: This is where you'll find the most authentic island music.

name: Herby's Sports Nights
description: French Harbour's sports bar comes alive on game nights with big screens, wings, and a rowdy crowd of fans.
insiderTip: Champions League and NFL games draw the biggest crowds.

name: Bariloche Live Music
description: Wine bar hosting live music every Wednesday and Friday night. The newest addition to Roatan's live music scene.
insiderTip: Wednesday and Friday are the nights to come - arrive by 7pm for a seat.

name: Tequila Jacks Pool Party
description: Weekend pool parties at Brisas del Mar with DJs, tequila specials, and a lively crowd around the pool.
insiderTip: Saturday pool party is the hottest day party in West End.
addressDescription: Brisas del Mar Hotel, West End

name: Coco Lounge Nights
description: Upscale lounge evenings in West Bay with craft cocktails, chill music, and a sophisticated beach atmosphere.
insiderTip: The best spot for a civilized evening with quality cocktails.

name: Beachers Sunday Funday
description: Legendary Sunday day-party at Beachers on West Bay Beach with live music, beach games, and all-day drink specials.
insiderTip: This is THE Sunday event on the island - locals and visitors alike.

name: Thirsty Turtle Live Music
description: Regular live music nights at the Thirsty Turtle on West Bay Beach with acoustic sets and a mellow vibe.
insiderTip: A more chill alternative to the West End party scene.

name: Eagle Ray's Game Night
description: Darts, pool, and trivia nights at this beloved dive bar. A low-key night out with the expat community.
insiderTip: Tuesday trivia night is fiercely competitive and a ton of fun.

name: Jolly Roger Pirate Night
description: Themed pirate parties with rum buckets, costume contests, and a rowdy good time at this West End favorite.
insiderTip: Wear your best pirate costume for drink discounts.

name: Punta Gorda Garifuna Nights
description: Authentic Garifuna drumming and cultural performances in the traditional village of Punta Gorda. A true cultural experience.
insiderTip: This is the real deal - authentic Garifuna culture you won't find anywhere else.

name: Half Moon Bay Full Moon Party
description: Monthly full moon parties on Half Moon Bay Beach with bonfires, music, and dancing under the moonlight.
insiderTip: Check the lunar calendar and plan around the full moon.
addressDescription: Half Moon Bay Beach, West End

name: Happy Harry's Live Band Night
description: Live band performances with a fun crowd, strong drinks, and a mix of rock, reggae, and Caribbean beats.
insiderTip: The house band always gets the crowd going.

name: Bodden Tours
description: The original Best of Roatan tour operator offering private guides, Zip-n-Dip excursions, ATV tours, and monkey/sloth sanctuary visits.
insiderTip: The Best of Roatan combo tour is the most popular for a reason.
addressDescription: Meeting point at cruise port, Coxen Hole

name: Gumbalimba Park
description: 20-acre beachfront nature park with Capuchin monkeys, exotic birds, iguanas, canopy ziplines, and lush botanical trails.
insiderTip: Feed the monkeys and parrots - they're incredibly friendly.

name: Daniel Johnson's Monkey & Sloth Hangout
description: Beloved animal sanctuary in French Cay where you can interact with sloths, capuchin monkeys, macaws, and parrots up close.
insiderTip: Note: since 2025 guests can no longer hold sloths, but you can still get close.
addressDescription: French Cay, 25 min from cruise port

name: South Shore Canopy Zipline
description: Adrenaline-pumping zipline park with 13 lines up to 1,500 feet long and speeds up to 60 mph through the jungle canopy.
insiderTip: The Superman line is the longest at 1,300 feet - pure adrenaline.

name: Charlie's Roatan Tours
description: Private and group island tours covering the best of Roatan including beaches, wildlife, culture, and local cuisine.
insiderTip: Charlie knows every hidden gem on the island.
addressDescription: Pickup from cruise port or hotels

name: Ruthless Roatan Charters
description: Deep sea and reef fishing charters departing from West End. Catch mahi-mahi, wahoo, and tuna in the deep blue Caribbean.
insiderTip: The half-day reef fishing trip is great for beginners.

name: Rony's Independent Tours
description: Local guide offering private tours to Daniel Johnson's sanctuary, ziplines, beaches, and cultural sites across the island.
insiderTip: Rony's personalized service makes every tour feel special.

name: Roatan Chocolate Factory
description: Tour the chocolate-making process from cacao bean to bar and sample delicious Honduran chocolate. A sweet island experience.
insiderTip: The tasting at the end is generous - save room.

name: Roatan Rum Company
description: Craft rum distillery offering tours and tastings of locally produced rum made with Caribbean sugarcane.
insiderTip: The aged rum tasting flight is worth every penny.

name: Mangrove Kayak Tours
description: Guided kayak tours through the mangrove tunnels of Oak Ridge. See incredible birdlife and marine ecosystems up close.
insiderTip: The morning tour has the best light and wildlife activity.

name: Roatan Fishing Charters
description: Full-day and half-day deep sea fishing trips targeting marlin, mahi-mahi, wahoo, and yellowfin tuna.
insiderTip: The full-day trip goes further offshore for the big game fish.

name: Horseback Riding Roatan
description: Beach horseback riding along scenic trails and sandy shores with beautiful views of the Caribbean.
insiderTip: The sunset beach ride is the most romantic option.

name: Punta Gorda Cultural Tour
description: Authentic Garifuna cultural experience in Honduras' oldest village. Traditional drumming, dancing, cooking, and history.
insiderTip: The cassava bread making demonstration is fascinating.

name: Discover Roatan Excursions
description: Cruise ship excursion specialists offering snorkel, beach, zipline, and combination tours from the port.
insiderTip: Book in advance online for the best prices.

name: Real Deal Roatan Tours
description: Locally operated tour company with a wide range of activities from snorkeling to ziplines to island-wide excursions.
insiderTip: Their combination tours give you the most bang for your buck.
addressDescription: Pickup from cruise port or hotels

name: Maya Key Private Island
description: Private 12-acre island with pristine beaches, an animal sanctuary, and superb snorkeling. A unique day-trip paradise.
insiderTip: The all-inclusive day pass is worth every penny for the island experience.

name: Little French Key
description: Private island paradise on Roatan's south side with white sandy beaches, crystal-clear waters, and a luxurious beach club.
insiderTip: Book the VIP cabana for the ultimate private island experience.

name: Roatan PaddleSports Tours
description: Guided paddleboard and kayak tours exploring West End's coastline, mangroves, and marine life from above.
insiderTip: The sunset paddle tour is magical.

name: Roatan Tucan Adventures
description: Tour operator offering monkey and sloth encounters, shuttle services, and combination island excursions.
insiderTip: Their shuttle to Daniel Johnson's is the most affordable option.

name: Waves of Art Gallery
description: Colorful gallery in West End showcasing local artists with unique handmade goods. A walking tour of Roatan's art scene.
insiderTip: The recycled island art pieces make the most unique souvenirs.

name: Macaw Container Market
description: Vibrant shopping village with handmade products, crafts, convenience stores, bars, and restaurants all housed in colorful containers.
insiderTip: Browse the handmade jewelry stalls - great prices for unique pieces.

name: Town Center at Port of Roatan
description: Duty-free shopping mall at the cruise port with jewelry, souvenirs, clothing, and international brands.
insiderTip: Duty-free prices on jewelry are actually decent deals.

name: Coxen Hole Municipal Market
description: Authentic local market selling fresh produce, Garifuna crafts, lenca pottery, straw baskets, and traditional Honduran goods.
insiderTip: This is where locals shop - great for authentic souvenirs at local prices.

name: Waves of Art
description: Eye-catching colorful gallery in West End popular with tourists. Local artists showcase unique handmade goods at affordable prices.
insiderTip: The recycled art pieces made from ocean debris are incredible.

name: Rusty Fish Recycled Island Art
description: Unique art gallery creating beautiful pieces from recycled materials found around the island. Eco-art at its best.
insiderTip: Every piece has a story - ask the artists about their process.

name: Wild Roatan
description: Conservation-focused shop selling eco-friendly clothing, reef-safe products, and items supporting marine conservation.
insiderTip: Every purchase supports reef conservation efforts.

name: Roatan Vibes Boutique
description: Local artisan craft shop featuring handmade jewelry, beachwear, and unique island-inspired gifts.
insiderTip: The handmade jewelry from local artisans makes the best gifts.

name: Mahogany Bay Shopping Village
description: Cruise port shopping area with souvenir shops, jewelry stores, and local craft vendors. First stop for cruise passengers.
insiderTip: Bargain with the craft vendors - prices are always negotiable.

name: Driftwood Designs
description: Home decor and gift shop in West End featuring handcrafted items from local materials including driftwood, shells, and coral.
insiderTip: The driftwood lamps are stunning conversation pieces.

name: Island Pearl Jewelry
description: Fine jewelry shop in French Harbour specializing in pearls, silver, and unique Caribbean-inspired pieces.
insiderTip: The pearl earrings are the best value for a special gift.

name: Caribbean Cigar Co.
description: Hand-rolled Honduran cigars in a relaxed lounge setting with premium cigars and cigar accessories.
insiderTip: Watch them roll cigars by hand - then smoke one on the deck.

name: Tucan Gift Shop
description: Local crafts, pottery, and Honduran souvenirs near the Dixon Cove area. Great for cruise ship passengers.
insiderTip: The hand-painted pottery makes unique gifts.

name: Roatan Art Gallery
description: Gallery showcasing paintings, photography, and mixed media from island artists. Beautiful pieces capturing Caribbean life.
insiderTip: The sunset photography prints are the most popular items.

name: Cariloha Bamboo
description: Bamboo clothing and lifestyle brand store at Mahogany Bay with soft, sustainable fabrics and resort wear.
insiderTip: The bamboo t-shirts are incredibly soft and great for the tropics.

name: Coral Stone Shopping Mall
description: Sandy Bay shopping center with restaurants, boutiques, and service shops serving the local community.
insiderTip: A good stop for everyday needs beyond tourist shops.

name: West Bay Beach Gift Shop
description: Beachside souvenir shop with t-shirts, magnets, postcards, and beach accessories right on West Bay.
insiderTip: Grab a Roatan t-shirt and reef-safe sunscreen before hitting the beach.

name: Island Surf & Board Shop
description: Surf shop in West End selling boards, gear, beachwear, and water sports equipment.
insiderTip: Good selection of reef-safe sunscreen and rash guards.

name: Honduras Coffee Co.
description: Specialty coffee shop and store selling premium Honduran single-origin beans, gifts, and brewing accessories.
insiderTip: Buy a bag of beans to take home - Honduran coffee is world-class.

name: Mega Plaza Mall
description: French Harbour's main shopping mall with restaurants, clothing stores, electronics, beauty salons, and everyday essentials.
insiderTip: Best place to stock up on everyday items at local prices.

name: Two Sisters Gift Shop
description: Local craft shop at Mahogany Bay selling traditional Honduran crafts, textiles, and handmade souvenirs.
insiderTip: The hand-woven textiles are beautiful and support local artisans.

name: Infinity Bay Resort
description: Premier West Bay resort with beachfront condos, multiple pools, dive center, and the famous Sunday beach party.
insiderTip: Book an oceanfront room for direct beach access and reef snorkeling.

name: Mayan Princess Beach & Dive Resort
description: Luxury all-inclusive resort on West Bay Beach with spacious condos, dive center, and full resort amenities.
insiderTip: The all-inclusive option is great value if you plan to eat at the resort.

name: Kimpton Grand Roatan
description: The most upscale hotel in West Bay with modern rooms, private balconies, reliable AC, and boutique luxury service.
insiderTip: The rooftop bar has the best panoramic views of any hotel on the island.

name: Arca Roatan
description: Intimate luxury boutique with only 16 rooms, saltwater pool, on-site restaurant, yoga classes, and beachfront access.
insiderTip: With only 16 rooms it feels like your own private resort.

name: Bananarama Dive Resort
description: Budget-friendly beachfront resort on West Bay Beach with a dive center, Vintage Pearl restaurant, and steps to La Placita.
insiderTip: Best budget option directly on West Bay Beach.

name: The Beach House
description: Boutique hotel considered the best place to stay in West End with Mila del Mar restaurant and stunning Half Moon Bay views.
insiderTip: Spring for the oceanview room - Half Moon Bay is your front yard.

name: Brisas del Mar
description: Mid-range hotel in West End with private pool, bar, restaurant, and walking distance to all restaurants and bars.
insiderTip: Perfect home base - everything in West End is walkable.

name: Tranquilseas Eco Lodge & Dive Center
description: Eco-friendly lodge in Sandy Bay with the Sunken Fish restaurant, dive center, and beautiful rooftop sunset views.
insiderTip: The rooftop sunset terrace is the best spot in Sandy Bay.

name: Seagrape Plantation Resort
description: Established resort in West End with 15+ years of operation, on-site dive shop, and a loyal following of return visitors.
insiderTip: The resort's dive shop has unmatched local reef knowledge.

name: Luna Beach Luxury Boutique Resort
description: Boutique beachfront resort between West End and West Bay with a dive shop, restaurant, and direct beach access.
insiderTip: Best location on the island - walk to both West End and West Bay.
addressDescription: Between West End and West Bay

name: Anthony's Key Resort
description: Iconic Sandy Bay resort famous for dolphin encounters, dive center, and a true Caribbean island experience.
insiderTip: The dolphin dive is a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

name: Blue Bahia Resort
description: Beachfront resort in Sandy Bay with the Beach Grill restaurant, weekly specials, and a laid-back island vibe.
insiderTip: The beachfront rooms have direct access to great snorkeling.

name: Roatan Backpackers' Hostel
description: Sandy Bay hostel with family rooms, private bathrooms, pool, and restaurant. Great budget option with a social vibe.
insiderTip: The pool area is where you'll meet fellow travelers.

name: Paradise Beach Hotel
description: West Bay beachfront hotel with PADI 5-Star dive center, restaurant, and direct access to world-class snorkeling.
insiderTip: Shore diving right off the hotel beach is incredible.

name: CoCo View Resort
description: Legendary dive resort on the South Shore with the famous Dockside Dive Center and spectacular wall diving.
insiderTip: The wall dive right off the resort is world-famous.

name: Las Verandas Hotel & Villas
description: Luxury hotel at Pristine Bay with Caribbean and reef views, modern-classic rooms, private terraces, and pools.
insiderTip: The views from the hilltop villas are breathtaking.

name: Posada Arco Iris
description: Colorful budget-friendly B&B in West End with simple rooms, breakfast included, and a friendly local atmosphere.
insiderTip: The included breakfast saves money and the location is perfect.

name: Camp Bay Eco Lodge
description: Remote eco-lodge on the quiet east end of the island with solar power, nature trails, and an untouched beach.
insiderTip: If you want to truly disconnect, this is the place.

name: Captain Van's Rentals
description: Motorcycles, scooters, mopeds, and bicycles available for rent. The most popular scooter rental on the island.
insiderTip: The scooter is the best way to explore the island on your own.

name: Discover Roatan Rent A Car
description: Car rental at the airport with a range of vehicles from economy to SUVs. Reserve online for best rates.
insiderTip: Book online in advance - airport pickup makes it seamless.

name: Atlantis Rent a Car
description: Innovative white-glove car rental service with hotel delivery and premium vehicles.
insiderTip: Their delivery service brings the car right to your hotel.

name: Thomas Car Rental
description: Short and long-term car rentals with economy, performance, and spacious vehicle options.
insiderTip: Their economy cars are the cheapest on the island.

name: Roatan PaddleSports Rentals
description: Paddleboard and kayak rentals from the West End waterfront. Hourly and daily rates available.
insiderTip: Sunrise paddleboarding in Half Moon Bay is pure magic.

name: West Bay Kayak Rental
description: Kayak and paddleboard rentals right on West Bay Beach with hourly and half-day options.
insiderTip: Paddle out to the reef for snorkeling from your kayak.

name: Captain Bob's Boat Rental
description: Small boat and panga rentals for self-guided reef exploration, fishing, and island hopping.
insiderTip: Rent a boat and find your own private beach - they'll show you the best spots.

name: Reef Rider Jet Ski Rentals
description: Jet ski rentals on West Bay Beach with guided tours and free-ride options. Safety briefing included.
insiderTip: The guided jet ski tour to the shipwreck is a blast.

name: Island Bicycle Rental
description: Beach cruisers and mountain bikes for rent in West End. The most eco-friendly way to explore the west side.
insiderTip: A beach cruiser is perfect for the West End to West Bay ride.

name: Sandy Bay Golf Cart Rentals
description: Golf cart rentals in Sandy Bay - the fun and easy way to get around the western side of the island.
insiderTip: A golf cart makes the Sandy Bay to West End trip a breeze.

name: Roatan Ferry Terminal (Galaxy Wave)
description: Ferry service connecting Roatan to La Ceiba on the mainland via the Galaxy Wave. Multiple daily departures.
insiderTip: Book the early morning ferry for the calmest crossing.

name: West Bay Water Taxi
description: Water taxi service between West End and West Bay Beach. The scenic and fun way to travel between the two areas.
insiderTip: Only $3-5 per person and way more fun than driving.
addressDescription: West End dock to West Bay Beach

name: Roatan Five Stars Tours & Transport
description: Private transport and excursion service with comfortable vehicles, English-speaking drivers, and island-wide coverage.
insiderTip: Book through WhatsApp for the fastest response.

name: Roatan Airport Transfers
description: Reliable airport pickup and drop-off service with meet-and-greet at the terminal. Pre-booking recommended.
insiderTip: Pre-book online to avoid the taxi hustle at the airport.

name: Cruise Port Shuttle Express
description: Shuttle service from Mahogany Bay and Port of Roatan cruise terminals to West Bay Beach, West End, and popular attractions.
insiderTip: The shuttle to West Bay is cheaper than a taxi.

name: East End Colectivo
description: Shared minibus service running the main road from Coxen Hole to the east end. The cheapest transport on the island.
insiderTip: Flag one down from the main road - they stop anywhere.

name: Oak Ridge Water Taxi
description: Water taxi service around Oak Ridge and the mangrove channels. The traditional way to get around this historic waterfront town.
insiderTip: Take the water taxi through the mangrove channel - it's a mini-tour itself.

name: VIP Transport Roatan
description: Premium private transport with luxury vehicles, professional drivers, and air-conditioned comfort for the whole island.
insiderTip: Worth the upgrade for the AC alone on hot days.

name: Roatan Taxi Service
description: Island-wide taxi service with set fares between major destinations. Available at the airport, ports, and by phone.
insiderTip: Always confirm the fare before getting in.

name: West End to French Harbour Bus
description: Local bus service connecting West End through Sandy Bay, Coxen Hole, and on to French Harbour. Runs regularly during the day.
insiderTip: The cheapest way to get to French Harbour for Gio's king crab.

name: West Bay Beach
description: Roatan's most famous beach with powdery white sand and crystal-clear turquoise water. World-class snorkeling right off shore.
insiderTip: Walk to the far south end for a quieter experience away from the resorts.

name: Half Moon Bay Beach
description: Beautiful crescent-shaped beach in West End with white sand, turquoise water, and protected waters perfect for beginner snorkelers.
insiderTip: The crescent shape protects the bay - great for calm swimming.

name: West End Beach
description: Relaxed beach along the West End strip popular with backpackers and divers. Great for kayaking and paddleboarding.
insiderTip: Rent a kayak and paddle along the coastline at sunset.
addressDescription: Along the main road, West End

name: Tabyana Beach
description: Rated among the best beaches in the Caribbean with close reef proximity, clear waters, and abundant coconut trees.
insiderTip: The reef is so close you can snorkel to it in minutes.
addressDescription: West Bay, near the south end

name: Camp Bay Beach
description: The longest completely natural beach on Roatan at the quiet east end. Stunning views, cool breezes, and almost no crowds.
insiderTip: Worth the drive for a true deserted beach experience.
addressDescription: Camp Bay, east end of island

name: Sandy Bay Beach
description: Quiet public beach in the Sandy Bay community with calm waters and a local atmosphere away from the tourist crowds.
insiderTip: A great spot to experience the beach like a local.

name: Beach Club Roatan (Palm Beach)
description: Spacious beach club with one of the finest white sand beaches on the island. Horseback riding, snorkeling, jet skiing, and kayaking available.
insiderTip: The horseback ride on the beach is unforgettable.

name: Pristine Bay Beach
description: Exclusive beach at Pristine Bay with soft white sand, gentle waves, pickleball, tennis, and outstanding snorkeling.
insiderTip: The pickleball courts right by the beach are a unique bonus.

name: Punta Gorda Beach
description: Beach near the historic Garifuna village of Punta Gorda with cultural significance and authentic local food nearby.
insiderTip: Combine a beach day with a Garifuna cultural tour.

name: Big Bight Beach
description: Hidden local beach at Flowers Bay on the south side with a quiet atmosphere and beautiful views.
insiderTip: One of the least visited beaches - feels like your own private cove.

name: Mahogany Bay Beach
description: Manmade beach at the Carnival cruise port with 20 acres to explore, lounge chairs, food, and water activities.
insiderTip: If you're on a cruise, the beach pass is the easiest option.

name: San Simon Beach Club
description: Upscale beach club in West Bay combining beachside luxury with exquisite dining and vibrant social settings.
insiderTip: The day pass includes lounge chair, towel, and pool access.
